scope: Entire vskill codebase — violations of principle: never manually edit ~/.claude plugin registry files
key_observation: No installed_plugins.json violations found — the file is never referenced in this codebase. All violations involve settings.json and plugin cache directory manipulation. src/settings/settings.ts is the single root cause for all HIGH findings.

findings.title: writeSettings() — root enabler for all direct settings.json mutations
findings.category: Direct filesystem mutation of Claude Code settings
findings.description: writeSettings() writes JSON.stringify(settings) directly to ~/.claude/settings.json via writeFileSync. All other HIGH violations route through this function.
findings.impact: Bypasses atomicity, locking, and validation that Claude CLI performs. Race condition if Claude Code writes simultaneously.
findings.recommendation: Use `claude settings set` CLI or equivalent sanctioned API instead of raw filesystem writes.

findings.title: purgeStalePlugins() — bulk direct settings.json write in init and cleanup commands
findings.category: Direct filesystem mutation of Claude Code settings + cache
findings.description: Iterates enabledPlugins, deletes keys not backed by lockfile, writes settings.json directly. cleanup.ts also deletes ~/.claude/plugins/cache/ subdirectories via rmSync.
findings.impact: Bulk-mutates settings.json and cache without CLI delegation. Comment 'before Claude Code caches them' acknowledges the known race condition risk.
findings.recommendation: Replace per-plugin removals with `claude plugin uninstall <id>`. For cache cleanup, require Claude Code to be stopped first.

findings.title: removePlugin() — live settings.json + cache deletion in `vskill remove`
findings.category: Direct filesystem mutation of Claude Code settings + cache
findings.description: removeCommand() calls removePlugin() (direct settings.json write) then directly deletes the plugin cache directory via rmSync.
findings.impact: Bypasses `claude plugin uninstall`. Claude Code's in-memory plugin state can diverge from disk.

findings.title: enablePlugin() / disablePlugin() — exported dead code that directly mutates settings.json
findings.description: Both functions mutate enabledPlugins in settings.json directly. Currently exported but not called by any command (only in tests).
findings.impact: If ever wired up, bypass Claude CLI. Their existence is a latent violation.
findings.recommendation: Remove if unused. If needed, replace with CLI delegation.

findings.title: cleanPluginCache() — cache directory deletion in `vskill install` without CLI
findings.category: Direct filesystem mutation of Claude Code plugin cache
findings.description: Constructs ~/.claude/plugins/cache/{marketplace}/{pluginName} and calls rmSync() to remove stale cache entries without CLI delegation.
findings.impact: Deletes from Claude Code's plugin cache directory without going through the CLI.
findings.recommendation: Delegate to `claude plugin uninstall` for cache cleanup, or only run when Claude Code is confirmed stopped.

findings.title: preuninstall.cjs — nukes entire ~/.claude/plugins/cache/ on npm uninstall
findings.category: Direct filesystem mutation of Claude Code plugin cache
findings.description: npm preuninstall hook deletes the entire ~/.claude/plugins/cache/ directory recursively, not just vskill's own subdirectories.
findings.impact: Could destroy cache entries for other tools sharing the same directory.
findings.recommendation: Scope deletion to only vskill's own cache subdirectory.

findings.title: SKILL.md instructs users to manually edit settings.json
findings.description: Instructs users to manually add MCP server config to ~/.claude/settings.json or project .claude/settings.json.
findings.impact: Users following this documentation will bypass Claude Code's settings management.
findings.recommendation: Replace with `claude mcp add` CLI instruction.
